# NL tour-runner harness: scripted operation sequences with deterministic timing

## Context

Tree line **A4** of the #13158 decomposition — the trinity enabler: one scripted, deterministic NL-driven operation sequence serves as **the demo** (viewers watch it), **the e2e scenario** (#14591 replays it as a spec), and **the video take** (screen-record a run; no hand-driving). The operator's YouTube seed (2026-07-04) is this leaf's product justification; #14589/#14590's tour-mode ACs and #14591's replay AC all consume it.

## The Problem

Without a runner, every tour is ad-hoc glue: demos hand-roll their own sequencing, e2e re-implements the same choreography with test-local timing, and recordings drift from both. Three copies of one artifact is the drift machine. The runner makes the tour script THE single source: a JSON-first scene list executed identically in demo mode, spec mode, and recording mode.

## The Architectural Reality

- Operation substrate: the #14625 tools (`execute_dock_operation` returning post-op documents; `get_dock_topology` for assertions) — the runner composes THEM, never touches the dock model directly (no parallel mutation path, the standing guardrail).
- Determinism constraints: scene steps advance on **state/event settledness, never wall-clock sleeps** (the whitebox-e2e no-sleeps rule lifted into the runner) — timing configs affect PACE (inter-scene pauses for viewers), not CORRECTNESS (op ordering/settling).
- Placement candidates (structural-pre-flight at claim): the runner is app-consumable demo infrastructure — likely `apps/agentos/` module scope or an `examples/` shared util; NOT `ai/` (it runs App-side). Sibling sweep decides.
- Script shape: JSON-first `{scenes: [{title, caption, steps: [{op-descriptor | topology-assert | pause}]}]}` — no functions, no live refs (recordable, diffable, reviewable).

## The Fix

One PR: the tour-runner module + the script schema + one executable smoke script:
1. **Script schema + validator** (fail-closed: unknown step types rejected with the vocabulary; every op descriptor validated against the #14587 eight-op set).
2. **The runner**: executes a script against a live workspace via the NL tools; three modes — `demo` (paced, captions surfaced), `spec` (no pacing, assertions strict), `record` (paced, deterministic seed, reduced-motion OFF asserted).
3. **Settledness contract**: each op step completes on the returned post-op document matching an expected topology predicate (or an explicit `topology-assert` step) — never `setTimeout` correctness.
4. **Smoke script**: a 3-step sequence against `examples/dashboard/dock` proving all three modes on shipped ops.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Script schema documented + validator fail-closed (unknown steps/ops enumerated).
- [ ] Runner executes the same script byte-identically in `demo`/`spec`/`record` modes (mode changes pace/output, never order).
- [ ] Zero wall-clock correctness: settledness via post-op documents/topology asserts only (review-checkable).
- [ ] Smoke script green against `examples/dashboard/dock`; two consecutive runs produce identical operation logs (the determinism falsifier).
- [ ] Unit specs for schema validation + mode equivalence (custom playwright config).
- [ ] Cross-family review before merge.

## Out of Scope

- The demo scenes themselves (F3–F5, G2–G4 — scripts are content, this is the player).
- Recording tooling/ffmpeg (F7).
- Perspective/cross-window steps until their tools exist (A1 — the schema reserves the step types, marked unavailable-fail-closed).

## Related

Parent: #13158 (tree line A4) · consumes #14587/#14625 tools · consumed by #14589/#14590 tour modes + #14591 replay spec · sibling A3 (topology diff tool — the assert step's future upgrade).

## Contract Ledger Matrix (backfilled per PR #14912 review cycles 1–2)

The consolidated matrix for the script format, validator exports, runner API, log wire format and workspace advisory lives in [this ticket comment](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/14640#issuecomment-4931650337) and is normative for the shipped surfaces; this body section is the required in-body anchor.

## Evidence Residual (evidence-ladder disposition)

- **Open AC:** "smoke script green against `examples/dashboard/dock`" — the named LIVE page replay. **Not delivered by PR #14912** (which delivers L2 fixture-parity on the identical document topology + a live `dockdemo` childapp exercising the same seam).
- **Disposition:** L3 delivery rides #14591 (the whitebox-e2e leaf that consumes the runner + screenplay). This ticket stays OPEN until that live smoke lands; PR #14912 references it as `Refs`, not `Resolves`.

## Contract Ledger Matrix (consolidated — covers #14640 + #14661, backfilled per PR #14912 review cycle 1)

| Target Surface | Source of Authority | Behavior | Fallback / Edge Case | Docs | Evidence |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| `neo.tour.script.v1` script format | `src/ai/client/tourScript.mjs` module JSDoc (schema block) | JSON-first scene/step data: `op` / `pause` / `topology-assert` steps, `{path, equals, epsilon?}` predicates; reserved `perspective` / `cross-window` types | Unknown/reserved step types, unknown ops, non-finite numbers, sparse arrays, cycles, functions → fail-closed validator errors with vocabulary enumerated | module JSDoc | unit: validator suite (fail-closed cases incl. hostile JSON) |
| `validateTourScript` / `evaluateExpectations` exports | same module | Pure, dependency-free validation + predicate evaluation; number comparisons absorb IEEE noise (`NUMBER_EPSILON`, per-predicate override) | Empty vocabulary → op steps cannot validate (explicit error); absent paths → truthful mismatch, never a skip | module JSDoc | unit: epsilon + mismatch-reporting specs |
| `Neo.ai.client.TourRunner` public API | class JSDoc | `start() → {completed, errors, log}` structured in every outcome; modes `demo`/`record`/`spec` (pace never correctness); events `beat`/`scene`/`error`/`complete`; `record` requires `reducedMotion === false` probed by the surface | Live-seam failure (holder gone) → structured abort; concurrent `start()` → throw (programming error); destroy mid-pause → quiet partial log | class JSDoc | unit: preflight refusals, seam-failure abort, mode-identity, destroy path |
| Operation-log wire format | class JSDoc (determinism contract) | Every `op` entry carries the complete JSON-cloned `descriptor` + `applied` + assert outcomes; no timestamps — two runs of one script are log-identical in any mode | Pause entries mode-invariant (only the waiting differs) | class JSDoc | unit: descriptor-clone spec + two-run determinism + three-mode identity |
| Script `workspace` advisory block | screenplay module JSDoc | Surface-applied mount config (e.g. `autoHideRevealOnHover`), outside the op stream; schema-tolerated extra key | Absent block → platform defaults (click-reveal) | screenplay JSDoc + scene-3 caption narration | unit: advisory + caption-narration spec; live: hover armed in the dockdemo childapp |

- 2026-07-12T21:16:58Z @neo-fable-clio referenced in commit `0537ee5` - "test(dashboard): TourRunner L3 smoke — two-run log-identity replay on the dock example (#14640)

The runner + schema shipped with their unit floor; the remaining acceptance
criterion was the named-live-page replay: the smoke script green against
examples/dashboard/dock with two consecutive runs producing identical operation
logs (the determinism falsifier). This delivers it:

- MainContainer.runTourSpec(script): the tour-replay seam completing the
  holder-contract trio (write half / read half / replay) — composes a fresh
  app-side DockService + TourRunner per call in spec mode and returns the
  structured {completed, errors, log} result. Example-tier: the dockdemo
  construct-time composition, exposed on demand so the shipped dock example is
  tour-replayable without carrying a tour bar.

- DockTourSmokeNL.spec.mjs: loads the live page, drives runTourSpec twice over
  the Neural Link fixture with a SELF-RESTORING 4-step script (resize ->
  topology-assert -> pause -> restore; all three executable v1 step types), and
  asserts App-Worker truth: run-1 completed with both ops applied + every
  assertion passed; run-2 log deep-equals run-1; the committed document restored
  to the shipped proportions; zero page errors across the double replay.

Verified: NEO_E2E_PORT=8091 e2e config, 1 passed (3.5s) — both replay wire
payloads byte-equal.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <fable@anthropic.com>"
- 2026-07-12T21:17:30Z @neo-fable-clio cross-referenced by PR #15107
- 2026-07-12T22:10:03Z @neo-fable-clio referenced in commit `ef40d96` - "fix(dashboard): L3 oracle completeness — hydrated derived baseline + settled-projection boundary (#14640)

Cycle-2 review (Emmy's exact-head falsifier + Euclid's superseding correction)
exposed two false-positive paths in the L3 witness and one wording overclaim:

1. Derived baseline, full-document proof: the spec no longer hardcodes the
   shipped proportions as its oracle. A zero-op baseline replay reads the
   COMPLETE hydrated worker-owned document; the probe + exact-restore script is
   built FROM that baseline; after each run the entire settled document must
   deep-equal the baseline (epsilon-aware for IEEE split-size noise), plus an
   independent out-of-adapter read. The assertion-bearing log-entry count is
   guarded before .every() so an empty filtered set can never pass vacuously.

2. Settled-projection boundary: onDockZoneDocumentChange now RETAINS its
   deferred re-projection as MainContainer#refreshPromise (the landed dockdemo
   workspace precedent) and returns the refresh into the chain; runTourSpec
   awaits storage hydration before composing and the LATEST projection before
   resolving, then returns the settled document with the runner result. The
   page-survival verdict now covers the projection work, not just the commits.

3. Contract wording: runTourSpec is documented as an example-local replay
   ADAPTER consuming the two-part holder contract (read/write halves) — not a
   third contract member.

Verified: NEO_E2E_PORT=8091 e2e config, 1 passed (5.3s) — baseline read + two
replays, identical logs, complete-document restoration proven against the
hydrated baseline.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <fable@anthropic.com>"
